title: Oil Marketing Companies Demand Suspension of Taxes, Claim They Are Absorbing Fuel Price Cuts
subject: Chamber of Oil Marketing Companies (COMAC)
content: The Chamber of Oil Marketing Companies (COMAC) has raised an alarm over the financial stability of its members, asserting that oil marketing companies (OMCs) are currently bearing the brunt of recent fuel price reductions. The Chamber firmly disputes the notion that the government is the primary entity absorbing these costs, highlighting a growing tension within the downstream petroleum sector regarding price management.

According to COMAC, the current pricing regime is unsustainable for private distributors who are struggling to maintain operations while fuel prices at the pump remain under significant pressure. The Chamber noted that while consumers may observe marginal relief at the pumps, the underlying operational costs and exchange rate fluctuations are being offset directly by the profit margins of the OMCs, leading to what they describe as a state of financial 'bleeding' within the industry.

In response to these mounting fiscal challenges, the Chamber is formally calling on the government to intervene by suspending specific statutory taxes and levies on petroleum products. They argue that such a policy shift is necessary to stabilize the industry and ensure that marketing companies can continue to provide essential services without facing total financial collapse.

The call for tax suspension comes at a time of heightened economic sensitivity, as stakeholders across the energy sector continue to debate the most effective strategies to manage fuel inflation. COMAC maintains that without immediate policy intervention or temporary tax relief, the long-term viability of many indigenous oil marketing firms remains at significant risk.
caption: The Chamber of Oil Marketing Companies (COMAC) is calling for immediate government intervention to protect the downstream petroleum sector.
author: GNN Editorial Bot
GNN Editorial Bot

title: Parliament Approves New Infrastructure Bill Amidst Heavy Debate
author: GNN Staff Reporter
caption: Members of Parliament arriving for the emergency session earlier today. (Photo: GNN Images)
content: In a session that stretched late into the evening, Parliament has formally approved the controversial National Infrastructure Development Bill. The legislation, which promises to reshape the nation's transportation networks and energy grids over the next decade, passed by a narrow margin following intense debate from opposition benches.

The bill authorizes the immediate release of funds for several major projects, including the long-delayed coastal highway expansion and a new hydroelectric initiative in the northern regions. Supporters argue these investments are critical for modernizing the economy and creating jobs.

However, critics raised significant concerns regarding the financing structure of the proposed projects. The opposition shadow minister for finance warned that the debt mechanisms outlined in the bill could burden future generations. 'We are mortgaging our future for immediate political gains,' she stated during the heated floor debate.

Independent analysts suggest the immediate impact on the construction sector will be profound. The initial phase of funding is expected to create over 10,000 direct jobs within the first eighteen months. Yet, concerns remain about the capacity of local contractors to handle the scale of the proposed works without significant foreign intervention.
The President is expected to sign the bill into law later this week, paving the way for the immediate formation of the oversight committee mandated by the legislation.

pullQuote.text: This is not merely a bill about concrete and steel; it is a blueprint for our economic survival in the next century.
pullQuote.author: Hon. Minister of Finance

keyProvisions: Allocation of $2.5 billion for coastal highway expansion.
Establishment of the National Energy Modernization Fund.
Mandatory 40% local content requirement for all primary contracts.

title: Central Bank Announces Unexpected Interest Rate Hike
author: Economic Desk
caption: The Reserve Bank headquarters in the capital. (Photo: GNN Business)
content: In a move that surprised financial markets, the Central Bank has announced a 50-basis-point increase in the benchmark interest rate. The decision comes as policymakers struggle to contain rising inflationary pressures fueled by global supply chain disruptions.

The Governor of the Central Bank stated that the 'pre-emptive strike' was necessary to anchor inflation expectations and ensure long-term stability. 'We cannot allow price instability to erode the purchasing power of our citizens,' he remarked during the press conference.
Commercial banks are expected to raise their lending rates immediately, which analysts warn could slow down consumer spending and mortgage applications in the coming months.

title: National Team Secures Qualification for Continental Cup
author: Sports Correspondent
caption: The team celebrating after the final whistle. (Photo: GNN Sports)
content: The National Team produced a masterful performance to defeat their regional rivals 2-0, securing their spot in next year's Continental Cup. Two late goals from the captain silenced the hostile home crowd and sparked celebrations back home.
The head coach praised the team's resilience and tactical discipline. 'We stayed focused until the very end, and it paid off. This group of players deserves every bit of this success,' he said.
The draw for the group stages will take place next month, with the team currently ranked as a dark horse for the tournament title.

title: Local Tech Hub Secures Major International Investment
author: Tech Bureau
caption: Developers working at the hub's main campus. (Photo: GNN Tech)
content: A prominent local technology incubator has secured $50 million from a consortium of global venture capital firms. This investment represents one of the largest early-stage rounds in the region's history.

The funding will be used to scale existing startups and expand the hub's digital literacy programs across rural areas. 'Our goal is to turn this region into a global innovation powerhouse,' the CEO stated.
Industry experts believe this move will attract further foreign direct investment into the burgeoning local digital economy.

title: Agricultural Exports See Record High This Quarter
author: Business Desk
caption: Grain being loaded for export at the main port. (Photo: GNN Business)
content: The Ministry of Trade has reported a significant surge in agricultural exports, with record volumes of grain and cocoa leaving the country this quarter. The increase is attributed to improved port logistics and high global demand.

Farmers across the country are seeing better returns on their produce, leading to increased reinvestment in modern farming equipment. 'This is a turning point for our agricultural sector,' said a local farming cooperative leader.
However, some logistical bottlenecks remain at the inland rail yards, which the government promises to address in the next budget cycle.

title: New Cyber Security Taskforce Initiated by Ministry
author: National Security Correspondent
caption: The state-of-the-art command center. (Photo: GNN National)
content: In response to growing digital threats, the Ministry of National Security has launched a specialized Cyber Security Taskforce. The unit will be responsible for protecting critical national infrastructure and responding to high-level digital breaches.

The Taskforce consists of top ethical hackers and strategic analysts recruited from both the public and private sectors. 'Our mission is to ensure our national digital borders are as secure as our physical ones,' the Ministry spokesperson said.
Collaborations with international security agencies are already being formed to share threat intelligence in real-time.
